title: Cyclosorus abruptus
intro: Cyclosorus abruptus, also known as the abrupt fern or abrupt cyclosorus, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant is valued for its health benefits, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cyclosorus abruptus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in traditional herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, as well as in modern supplements, to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
taxonomy: Cyclosorus abruptus is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the marsh ferns or thelypterid ferns. This family belongs to the order Polypodiales, a group of ferns that are characterized by their two distinct types of sporangia. The order Polypodiales is further classified within the subclass Polypodiidae, which is a group of ferns that are typically found in a variety of habitats around the world. Within the subclass Polypodiidae, the class Polypodiopsida is the most inclusive group, encompassing a wide range of fern species. The classification of Cyclosorus abruptus is further refined within the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Cyclosorus abruptus is known to have various medicinal uses in traditional medicine, particularly in Asian cultures. Its leaves are used to treat fever, headache, and other inflammatory conditions. In some regions, the plant is also used to manage respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Additionally, the plant's extracts have been studied for their potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Cyclosorus abruptus for medicinal purposes.
